summary: Iteration 6 backend verification of the redesigned 'Breakout Confirmed' multi-factor logic on LIVE OKX data via https://futures-analyzer-4.preview.emergentagent.com. Pytest suite exercised /api/scan (1h cached), /api/scan (30m fresh), and 4 named setups (PENDLEUSDT, GOOGLUSDT, BTCUSDT, AIUSDT). All 9 iteration-5 invariants still hold across every setup emitted by both scans (44 setups total, 12 best + 10 preparing per scan). All 9 new breakout invariants (A)-(I) hold across every non-neutral setup and across every direct /api/setup call. Live sample even contained a natural A+ setup (ATUSDT SELL, conf 92) with breakout_analysis.confirmed=True + all 6 criteria True + display_checklist matching + confidence>=88 — invariants (C), (D), (H) all exercised on real data, not just vacuously. Confirmed rate on live scan is 3/22 = ~14%, which reads as well-calibrated (strict but not vacuous). One separate flakiness issue observed: right after a fresh scan, /api/setup/{symbol}?timeframe=1h intermittently returns 400 'Not enough data' — appears to be OKX rate-limit bleed-through, unrelated to the breakout redesign. Waiting 20-30s recovers.

A_breakout_analysis_present.example: ATUSDT/AMATUSDT/GOOGLUSDT/PENDLEUSDT/AIUSDT all carry breakout_analysis; only neutral setups may be null (invariant I).

B_schema_exact.example: Every ba has {confirmed:bool, reason:str, criteria:dict, retest_held:bool} and criteria carries all 6 required keys (body_close_beyond_level, volume_above_average, strong_momentum_candle, structure_confirms, price_still_accepted, htf_aligned), all bool.

C_dc_matches_confirmed.example: ATUSDT: dc['Breakout Confirmed']=True, ba.confirmed=True. PENDLEUSDT: dc=False, ba.confirmed=False. Verified over 44 scan setups + 4 named setups → 0 mismatches.

D_confirmed_true_all_criteria_true.example: 3 confirmed=True setups on 1h scan (ATUSDT, AMATUSDT, GOOGLUSDT); each has ALL 6 criteria True.

E_confirmed_false_reason_nonempty.example: PENDLEUSDT ba.reason='No candle body has closed above the key level yet'; AIUSDT ba.reason='No candle body has closed below the key level yet'. All 38 confirmed=False setups have non-empty reasons.

F_checklist_reasons_matches_reason.example: AIUSDT: checklist_reasons['Breakout Confirmed'] == breakout_analysis.reason == 'No candle body has closed below the key level yet'. ATUSDT (confirmed=True) also mirrors reason into checklist_reasons.

G_all_iteration_5_invariants_still_hold.example: 44 scan setups + 4 named setups: 0 iter5 violations. grade dist scan_1h={A+:1, A:13, B:8}; scan_30m_fresh={A+:1, A:16, B:5}. auto_rr always ∈ {1.5,2.0,2.5,3.0}, rr==auto_rr always. WAIT⇒conf≤82 always. Level ordering always correct per direction.

H_A_plus_requires_breakout_confirmed.example: ATUSDT (A+, SELL, conf=92): display_checklist all 6 True including Breakout Confirmed=True. ba.confirmed=True with all 6 criteria True. Real-world A+ exercised, not just vacuous.

I_neutral_may_have_null_breakout.example: No neutral setups with null ba observed in current scan; test allows null on neutral without failing.

top_reasons_when_false: No candle body has closed above the key level yet
No candle body has closed below the key level yet
weak candle (doji / small body); structure not LH-LL
weak candle (doji / small body); structure not HH-HL
No candle body has closed below the key level yet
No candle body has closed above the key level yet
volume below recent average; weak candle (doji / small body)

confirmed_breakdown.calibration_assessment: Well-calibrated. ~14% (3/22) of live setups pass the 6-factor Breakout Confirmed gate, and those that pass are strong (one is an A+ with confidence 92). The rule is strict enough to keep A+ rare but not so strict it never triggers — behaviourally correct for a 'multi-factor confirmation' signal.

endpoints_tested.GET /api/setup/PENDLEUSDT?timeframe=1h: 200 (long A, ba.confirmed=false, reason: 'No candle body has closed above the key level yet'). Occasionally 400 immediately after a fresh scan — see minor issue below.
endpoints_tested.GET /api/setup/GOOGLUSDT?timeframe=1h: 200 (long A, ba.confirmed=true, all 6 criteria True). Was known 'volume below average' case at spec time but has since evolved to confirmed=true.
endpoints_tested.GET /api/setup/BTCUSDT?timeframe=1h: 200 (varies by minute, matches spec 'No candle body has closed above the key level yet' case).
endpoints_tested.GET /api/setup/AIUSDT?timeframe=1h: 200 (short A, ba.confirmed=false, reason: 'No candle body has closed below the key level yet'; correctly short-direction wording).

minor.issue: Transient 400 'Not enough data' when called immediately after /api/scan?fresh=1 (observed for PENDLEUSDT / GOOGLUSDT / BTCUSDT / AIUSDT in a burst). Recovers after 20-30s wait. Symptom suggests OKX rate-limit bleed-through (klines fetch returning short series) and lack of a retry or safe-fallback in the setup path. Not related to breakout redesign. Recommend: add 1 retry-with-backoff on the OKX kline call, OR return the last cached setup with a warning field instead of a hard 400.

pytest_result: 19 collected. On a cold run: 19/19 PASS. On a run immediately after a heavy scan burst, 4/19 fail with HTTP 400 'Not enough data' from OKX rate-limit bleed on the /api/setup path (NOT a breakout logic failure — the same tests pass on a re-run 30s later). Breakout invariants themselves: 19/19 always PASS.
action_items: OPTIONAL/MEDIUM: Harden /api/setup/{symbol} against transient OKX rate-limit responses. Either retry the kline fetch once, or serve last-known data with a 'stale' marker, rather than returning 400 'Not enough data'. This makes the endpoint robust when the frontend refreshes a symbol right after a fresh scan.
OPTIONAL: Consider surfacing checklist_reasons for other checklist items too (not just Breakout Confirmed) — currently checklist_reasons is Breakout-only. If that's by design, ignore.
critical_code_review_comments: breakout_analysis is the single source of truth: display_checklist['Breakout Confirmed'] is populated from breakout_analysis.confirmed, and checklist_reasons['Breakout Confirmed'] is populated from breakout_analysis.reason. Zero divergence observed over 44+4 setups.

When confirmed=True the reason is a short human-readable positive summary (e.g. 'Body closed beyond level · volume surge · strong candle · LH-LL structure · level held'); when False it lists the failing factor(s) joined by '; '. Cleanly readable for a UI badge.
A+ path is exercised by real live data (ATUSDT SELL conf 92 with all 6 breakout criteria True). This is the first iteration in which the A+ contract is verified against a non-vacuous sample.
Iteration_5 A+ contract is still enforced: A+ requires structure_ok + trend_ok + all 6 display_checklist True + direction!=neutral + confidence>=88. All hold on ATUSDT.
auto_rr/risk_reward parity, level ordering per direction, WAIT clamp, entry_quality banding — all still hold post-breakout-redesign.

success_rate.backend: 100% for breakout-redesign invariants (A-I) + iter5 invariants on all endpoints when OKX is not rate-limiting. Only transient failures: HTTP 400 on /api/setup right after a fresh scan burst — separate issue, not breakout logic.

seed_data_creation: None. All checks against live OKX perpetual swap data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 backend breakout redesign is verified. A+ path was exercised on a real live setup (ATUSDT SELL conf 92) — first non-vacuous A+ verification. Only observed backend flakiness is /api/setup returning 400 'Not enough data' when called within seconds of a fresh /api/scan (OKX rate-limit bleed). If retesting, insert a 20-30s cool-off between scan-fresh and setup calls, or run the two suites separately. Frontend TradeCard was updated to render checklist_reasons under each item — worth a UI check on /analyze/PENDLEUSDT or /analyze/BTCUSDT to confirm reason string is visible under 'Breakout Confirmed'.
rca_of_issue: The only observed anomaly (400 'Not enough data' on /api/setup right after a fresh scan) is a rate-limit interaction with OKX, not a defect in the redesigned breakout logic. Repro: (1) call GET /api/scan?timeframe=30m&fresh=1 (this pulls klines for ~50 symbols); (2) within ~10s, call GET /api/setup/PENDLEUSDT?timeframe=1h; (3) endpoint returns 400 {'detail':'Not enough data'} because the internal OKX kline fetch is rate-limited and returns a truncated series that fails the min-length gate; (4) wait 20-30s → same call returns 200 normally. Mitigation: add exponential-backoff retry (1 attempt) around the OKX kline call in the setup path, or fall back to the last successfully cached kline series instead of raising 400.
